en: Creates a list of locations in the shape of a box (only the corners!).

Creates a list of vectors in the shape of a box including corners and edges. The density value controls the amount of points. A higher number increases the amount of points. A density of 1 corresponds to 1 point per block.
Creates a list of vectors in the shape of a box (only the corners!).

Creates a list of vectors in the shape of a box including corners and edges. The density value controls the amount of points. A higher number increases the amount of points. A density of 1 corresponds to 1 point per block.
Gives the reference frame of an entity. The reference frame is used to get locations with respect to the entities orientation.
Creates a list of locations in the shape of a cube (only the corners!). To get a list of locations including the cube edges use cube outline.

Creates a list of locations in the shape of a cube, including the corners and vertices of the cube. The density value controls the amount of points. A higher number increases the amount of points. A density of 1 corresponds to 1 point per block.

Creates a list of locations linking multiple locations with lines. The density value controls the amount of points. A higher number increases the amount of points. A density of 1 corresponds to 1 point per block.
Gives the midpoint between locations.
Moves a list of locations from one location to another.
Calculates the offset of locations from vectors.
Creates a list of locations in the shape of a polygon (only the corners!). If you want a polygon with edges use polygon outline.

Creates a list of locations in the shape of a polygon including corners and edges. The density value controls the amount of points. A higher number increases the amount of points. A density of 1 corresponds to 1 point per block.
Converts degree to radian
Yaw and pitch of a frame. Mainly used to rotate or incline reference frames
Gives locations in a cylindrical coordinate system.

Creates a list of locations in the shape of a helix. The step parameter determines how many blocks the helix will go upwards in one rotation. The density value controls the amount of points. A higher number increases the amount of points. A density of 1 corresponds to 1 point per block.
Creates a list of locations in the shape of a line. The density value controls the amount of points. A higher number increases the amount of points. A density of 1 corresponds to 1 point per block.

Gives a location on the line between two locations. The number parameter describes how far the point is from the first location. A value of 0 corresponds the the start (first location). A value of 1 corresponds to the end (location 2). 0.5 is the midpoint between the two locations.
Creates a reference frame from a yaw and a pitch.
Rotates locations around a rotation center and a rotation axis by an angle.
Rotates locations around a rotation center and rotation axis by an angle.
Scales locations from a center by a scaling factor. The direction parameter adds an additional scaling along the x-, y- and z-axis.

Creates a list of vectors in the shape of a polygon including corners and edges. The density value controls the amount of points. A higher number increases the amount of points. A density of 1 corresponds to 1 point per block.
Creates a list of vectors in the shape of a line. The density value controls the amount of points. A higher number increases the amount of points. A density of 1 corresponds to 1 point per block.

Creates a list of vectors in the shape of a line between the two given vectors. The density value controls the amount of points. A higher number increases the amount of points. A density of 1 corresponds to 1 point per block.

Creates a list of vectors linking multiple vectors with lines. The density value controls the amount of points. A higher number increases the amount of points. A density of 1 corresponds to 1 point per block.
Gives the midpoint between vectors.
Creates a list of vectors in the shape of a polygon (only the corners!). If you want a polygon with edges use polygon outline.

Creates a list of vectors in the shape of a polygon including corners and edges. The density value controls the amount of points. A higher number increases the amount of points. A density of 1 corresponds to 1 point per block.
Creates a list of vectors in the shape of a sphere. The locations are randomly distributed on the sphere surface.
Mirrors vectors in another vector. The direction parameter applies an additional scaling for each axis.
Rotates vectors around a rotation center and a rotation axis by an angle.
Rotates vectors around a rotation center and rotation axis by an angle.
Scales vectors by a scaling factor and an optional direction. The direction parameter adds an additional scaling along the x-, y- and z-axis.
Mirrors locations in another location. The direction parameter applies an additional scaling for each axis.
Creates a list of locations in the shape of a sphere. The density value controls the amount of points. A higher number increases the amount of points. A density of 1 corresponds to 1 point per block.
Gives locations in a spherical coordinate system.
Converts radian to degree
Creates a list of vectors in the shape of a cube (only the corners!). To get a list of locations including the cube edges use cube outline.
Offset vectors by other vectors.
Creates a list of vectors in the shape of a circle
Reference frame that can be used to get vectors relative to some direction.
Converts a vector in a reference frame to the standard Minecraft coordinates.
Creates a list of locations in the shape of a sphere. The locations are randomly distributed on the sphere surface.
Creates a path between the locations. The density value controls the amount of points. A higher number increases the amount of points. A density of 1 corresponds to 1 point per block.
Creates a list of vectors in the shape of a sphere. The density value controls the amount of points. A higher number increases the amount of points. A density of 1 corresponds to 1 point per block.
Creates a list of locations in the shape of a circle. The density value controls the amount of points. A higher number increases the amount of points. A density of 1 corresponds to 1 point per block.
Adds each offset to all vectors
Creates a path between vectors. The density value controls the amount of points. A higher number increases the amount of points. A density of 1 corresponds to 1 point per block.

Creates a list of vectors in the shape of a helix. The step parameter determines how many blocks the helix will go upwards in one rotation. The density value controls the amount of points. A higher number increases the amount of points. A density of 1 corresponds to 1 point per block.
